title: 10 Herbal Creams For Nausea
intro_desc: Herbal creams for nausea can be a lifesaver for people who experience frequent or severe nausea. But have you ever wondered why herbal teas relieve nausea so effectively? It's because these herbs contain special compounds that calm the stomach and reduce nausea. For example, Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help ease digestive discomfort. Ginkgo biloba, on the other hand, improves blood flow to the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting. And Cinnamomum verum, or cinnamon, has a soothing effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and calming nausea.  These herbs work by targeting the root causes of nausea, whether it's digestive issues, food poisoning, or even morning sickness during pregnancy. By using herbal teas or creams, you can alleviate nausea and enjoy everyday activities without feeling queasy. Imagine being able to eat your favorite foods, go for a walk, or even drive without feeling like you're going to vomit. Herbal creams for nausea can give you back your freedom and confidence. They can also help you avoid the side effects of prescription medications, which can be harsh on the body. By incorporating herbal remedies into your daily routine, you can find natural relief from nausea and take control of your health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce swelling in the stomach, making it a good treatment for nausea. Another compound, shogaol, also found in ginger, has been shown to have antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach from damage. The volatile oils in ginger, such as zingiberene and bisabolol, help to relax the muscles in the digestive system, reducing spasms that can cause nausea. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the stomach, Zingiber officinale creams can help to alleviate nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale root powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the Zingiber officinale root powder and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers or a tin. Let it cool and harden at room temperature for about 30 minutes.
Once hardened, label the containers and store them in a cool, dry place. Apply a small amount to the skin as needed to help with nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are powerful antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation in the body. These antioxidants, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system and alleviate nausea. Ginkgo biloba also contains bilobalide, a terpenoid that can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and prevent spasms that can cause nausea. Additionally, ginkgo biloba has a natural anti-emetic effect, which means it can help to prevent vomiting and relieve nausea. By using ginkgo biloba creams, you can benefit from its anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties to help alleviate nausea and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Steep the dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in 1 cup of hot water for 5 minutes, then strain and discard the leaves. Add the Ginkgo biloba infusion to the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify. Add peppermint essential oil and mix well. Your Ginkgo biloba cream for nausea is now ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams contains a compound called eugenol, a natural antispasmodic that helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and alleviate nausea. Another key component is cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the digestive tract and ease discomfort. The antioxidants in Cinnamomum verum, including cinnamic acid, help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to nausea and stomach upset. Additionally, the essential oils in Cinnamomum verum creams, such as linalool and beta-pinene, have a soothing effect on the digestive system and can help to calm nausea. By targeting the underlying causes of nausea, Cinnamomum verum creams may provide relief from this uncomfortable symptom.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Cinnamomum verum essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each heating.
Once the mixture is melted and smooth, remove it from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the Cinnamomum verum essential oil and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use. Apply as needed to help relieve nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains compounds like anethole and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can cause nausea. The anethole in Foeniculum vulgare also has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to alleviate nausea caused by stress or anxiety. The essential oils in the cream, including anethole and limonene, have a relaxing effect on the stomach muscles, which can help to prevent nausea. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare creams have been shown to have a soothing effect on the stomach lining, which can help to reduce nausea caused by stomach acid.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds, 1 cup of coconut oil, and a double boiler.
Heat the coconut oil in the double boiler until it reaches 160°F.
Add the Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the seeds. Let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and mix until melted and combined. Pour into containers and let cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains menthone, menthol, and limonene as its active constituents. These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce stomach discomfort and ease nausea. Menthone and menthol also have a sedative effect, which can help to calm the stomach and reduce spasms that may be contributing to nausea. Limonene has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing stomach discomfort and inflammation, and protecting the stomach lining, Mentha x piperita creams can help to alleviate nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and a few drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in the peppermint essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or a mold to shape the cream.
Let the cream cool and solidify, then store it in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams contains a high concentration of piperine, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can be a contributing factor to nausea. Piperine also has antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, further alleviating nausea symptoms. The cream also contains other beneficial compounds, such as limonene and beta-caryophyllene, which have been shown to have anti-emetic properties. By targeting these underlying causes, Piper nigrum creams may help to provide relief from nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 tablespoons of Piper nigrum oil, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and add Piper nigrum oil, vitamin E oil, and peppermint essential oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or container. Let it cool and harden for 30 minutes.
Once hardened, your Piper nigrum cream is ready to use. Apply as needed to help alleviate nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains a high amount of boiactive compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate. These constituents have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and calming effects on the body, which can help alleviate nausea. The linalool in Lavandula angustifolia creams has been found to have a sedative effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause nausea. The anti-inflammatory properties of linalyl acetate can also help soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort associated with nausea. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia creams can provide relief from nausea and other related symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the dried flowers. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil to the liquid. Stir well.

Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan. Heat over low heat until melted. Then, add the liquid mixture and stir until combined. Pour into small containers and let cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful antioxidant. Curcumin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stomach irritation, a common cause of nausea. Turmeric also contains a compound called demethoxycurcumin, which has been shown to stop nausea-causing chemicals in the stomach from working. Another compound in turmeric, bisdemethoxycurcumin, has been found to have a direct effect on the area of the brain that regulates nausea. These properties in Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate nausea by soothing the stomach and brain.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in the double boiler over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) and 1 tablespoon of ginger powder to the melted mixture.
Continue heating the mixture until it reaches 120°F to 140°F (49°C to 60°C). Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool and solidify. Once solid, store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains essential oils that help with nausea. The main active constituents are borneol, bornyl acetate, and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, which can contribute to nausea. The aroma of rosemary essential oil also has a stimulating effect on the brain, which can help to improve digestion and reduce nausea. When applied topically, the rosemary oil in the cream may help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 10 grams of dried Rosmarinus officinalis in a small saucepan.
Heat the distilled water over low heat and add the dried Rosmarinus officinalis. Let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Combine the strained liquid with coconut oil in a small bowl. Let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, pour the mixture into a clean container and store it in the fridge. Use 1-2 teaspoons as needed to help alleviate nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is also found in licorice root. This compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea. Glycyrrhizin also has a soothing effect on the stomach, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contain flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause nausea. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help reduce nausea by calming the stomach and reducing inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for nausea
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of glycerin, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until melted.
In a separate bowl, mix 1 cup of distilled water with 1/2 cup of glycerin and 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder until the powder is fully dissolved.
Combine the melted beeswax with the water-glycerin mixture and stir until well combined.
Add 10 drops of peppermint essential oil and pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and harden before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea might help prevent nausea. Ginger tea, in particular, is often used for its calming effect on the stomach. The soothing properties of herbal teas like peppermint and chamomile can also ease digestive discomfort, potentially reducing the likelihood of nausea. This is because these teas can help slow down digestion and calm stomach muscles.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for nausea can be a gentle solution, but consuming them daily can be a mixed bag. While some herbs like ginger and peppermint are safe and might help, others like chamomile and licorice root can interact with medications or worsen conditions like high blood pressure when taken frequently. Moderation is key.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like ginger and peppermint can help with nausea. Results usually start within 15 to 30 minutes after drinking the tea. Some people feel better faster, while others may take a bit longer. The effectiveness of herbal teas can vary from person to person, but they're often a good starting point for relief.
faq_time: In the morning, try drinking herbal tea for nausea. It can help settle your stomach after a night of poor digestion. Many herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, can ease nausea and help you feel more comfortable. Drinking it first thing helps your body start the day feeling better.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for nausea. For example, peppermint tea might make nausea worse in people who take certain medications that slow stomach emptying. Ginger tea can also affect how well certain medications work. It's best to check the label or talk to the pharmacist about your specific situation.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be careful with herbal teas. Some can cause problems, like peppermint, which can slow down digestion. Ginger tea is generally safe, as it can help with nausea, but check the ingredients and look for products made from pure ginger root. Always choose a reputable brand.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for nausea can be a good option for breastfeeding women, but be cautious. Some herbs, like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile, are commonly used and considered safe. However, others like sage and pennyroyal may not be suitable. Research the herbs you plan to use and choose ones with a proven track record of safety for breastfeeding mothers.
